pr.title: Remove unions from Fighter struct, preferring bitfields where appropriate
pr.author: r-burns

summary: Converted several Fighter struct byte/bitfield union overlays into direct bitfield members or named array fields, then updated fighter code to use those fields instead of manual shifts, masks, raw byte aliases, or M2C_FIELD accesses. The largest change was in src/melee/ft/types.h, with follow-up cleanup in common fighter code, collision, action command parsing, Marth counter effects, Game & Watch init, and attack combo interrupt logic. The PR also resolved at least one decompilation TODO around ft_data->x54 and simplified some matching-sensitive code by moving repeated Marth effect cleanup into a static inline helper.
what_changed: Removed multiple anonymous unions in Fighter-related structs where a raw byte alias existed only to expose individual bitfields.
Flattened Fighter bitfields around offsets 0x2218, 0x2219, 0x2221 and similar locations so code can reference fields such as allow_interrupt, x2219_b0, x221B_b5, and x221D_b6 directly.
Changed x2220_flag from a union containing both named bitfields and a raw flags byte into a named struct of bitfields with b0 as a 3-bit field and b3-b7 as single-bit fields.

Replaced manual bit tests like ((fp->x221D >> 1) & 1), ((fp->x2218 >> 7) & 1), and shifts of x2220_flag.flags with direct bitfield reads such as fp->x221D_b6, fp->allow_interrupt, fp->x2220_flag.b5, and fp->x2220_flag.b6.
Added a concrete ftData field at offset 0x54, declared as int x54, replacing part of an opaque filler range.

In ftCo_8009F834, replaced a previous M2C_ERROR/TODO expression using M2C_FIELD(temp_r30->ft_data, s32*, 0x54) and x2220_flag.flags bit manipulation with temp_r30->ft_data->x54 + temp_r30->x2220_flag.b0 and direct increment/reset of the 3-bit b0 field.
In ftGw_Init_OnLoad, replaced access through the removed x614 alias with fp->x610_color_rgba[1] when storing the Game & Watch outline color.

In ftAction_80072B3C, simplified action command extraction by assigning fp->x2100 from a shifted signed int and assigning fp->x2101_bits_8 directly instead of using M2C_FIELD to preserve and patch a raw x2101 byte.

In ftMs_SpecialLw.c, introduced static inline ftMs_SpecialLw_80139140_inline to contain the effect-spawn and callback-clearing logic, replacing an inline block that previously included a raw x2219 access marked as required for regalloc.
Simplified OSReport in ftCo_8009F834 to pass only the argument used by the format string.
smart_moves: Kept bitfield names offset-oriented, e.g. x2219_b0 and x221D_b6, when semantic names were not established, avoiding invented meaning while still eliminating unsafe raw-byte union access.
Used an existing semantic field name, allow_interrupt, where the bit's meaning was already known, improving readability in ftCo_AttackS42_IASA.
Preserved layout documentation with offset comments beside each bitfield, which is important when removing unions from a decompilation-critical struct.
Converted raw bit manipulation into direct bitfield operations only where the struct layout supported it, reducing M2C-style clutter without broad behavior changes.
Used a static inline helper in ftMs_SpecialLw.c to isolate matching-sensitive code generation while removing the previous artificial raw x2219 read used for register allocation.
Turned an opaque ftData padding region into an explicit x54 field once call sites demonstrated that offset's use, removing an M2C_ERROR/TODO and improving type knowledge.
decomp_lessons: For large structs such as Fighter, raw-byte union aliases are not always worth keeping; direct bitfields can make call sites cleaner and can eliminate repetitive shifts and masks when the accessed bits are stable.
When replacing byte aliases with bitfields, update all call sites together so removed fields such as x2218, x2219, x221B, x221D, x2101, x614, or x2220_flag.flags do not linger in code.
Offset comments are essential when flattening unions: they preserve auditability of byte and bit positions after the syntactic structure changes.
M2C_FIELD and M2C_ERROR expressions often point to missing type information; once the underlying offset is understood, adding a named field can unblock a cleaner matched decompilation.

Bitfield numbering in this codebase may not visually match the manual shift expression names; for example prior code using shifts of x221D was rewritten to x221D_b6, so future edits should trust verified layout comments and matching results rather than infer names casually.
Inline helpers can be a useful matching tactic when a previous local block relied on artificial reads or awkward variable separation for register allocation.
When replacing union aliases for color or array overlays, prefer the canonical storage form used by the struct, as with x610_color_rgba[1] replacing x614.
naming_conventions: Unknown Fighter bitfields retain offset-plus-bit names such as x2072_b0, x2219_b0, x221B_b5, x221D_b6, and x2221_b7.
Known semantic bits can use meaningful names, as shown by allow_interrupt at fp+2218:0.
Packed bitfield groups may remain named as structs when useful, e.g. x2220_flag with members b0, b3, b4, b5, b6, and b7.
Newly identified but semantically unknown struct fields may use offset names, e.g. ftData::x54.
Array-style canonical names are preferred over overlapping scalar aliases when the storage is fundamentally an array, e.g. x610_color_rgba[1] instead of x614.
Matching helper functions introduced only to shape codegen can use a symbol-derived suffix such as ftMs_SpecialLw_80139140_inline.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced manual byte-mask code with direct C bitfield access to produce cleaner source while preserving intended packed-byte behavior.
Changed x2220_flag.flags read/modify/write sequences into increment and reset of the 3-bit x2220_flag.b0 field.
Removed a raw x2219 read that was previously annotated as required for regalloc by moving the surrounding logic into a static inline helper.
Adjusted local padding in ftMs_SpecialLw_80139140 from u8 unused1[12] to u8 unused1[4], suggesting stack layout was retuned after the inline extraction.
Used signed shifting in ftAction_80072B3C, including *(int*) cmd->x8 << 7 and cmd_x8 >> 7, to express the action command sign/field extraction more directly.
Simplified an OSReport call to match its format string, removing extra unused floating-point arguments.
review_feedback: No review comments were included in the provided PR slice.
The context reports one issue comment, but no human text excerpt was available, so reviewer intent or discussion cannot be assessed from this slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching Fighter bitfields near offsets 0x2218-0x2221 to see whether more semantic names replaced xNNNN_bN placeholders.
Search for remaining M2C_FIELD uses in fighter code that may indicate missing struct fields like ftData::x54.
Search for remaining raw-byte aliases or unions in src/melee/ft/types.h that could be safely converted to direct bitfields.
Inspect later changes to ftMs_SpecialLw_80139140_inline to determine whether the inline helper remained necessary for matching.
Audit uses of x2220_flag.b0 to confirm its 3-bit counter semantics and whether it deserves a semantic name.
